vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1408: Graphify graph drifts because no automation enforces updates — make graphify update automatic via postMergeLifecycle hook, with a pan doctor staleness check as safety net.

plan.title: Graphify graph drifts because no automation enforces updates — make graphify update automatic or remove the rule
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: CLAUDE.md tells work agents to read graphify-out/GRAPH_SUMMARY.md (~700 tokens) for orientation on every task start, and to run `graphify update .` after editing code. The first rule is enforced by agent reflex; the second is not — no PostToolUse hook, no post-merge automation, no close-out step. As of 2026-05-23, GRAPH_SUMMARY.md on origin/main was last updated 2026-05-11 (12 days stale). Every agent in that 12-day window paid 700 tokens for orientation data describing a codebase that no longer exists, then wrote code against false assumptions that downstream review/test/ship had to catch. The graph is supposed to be a high-leverage helper; without enforcement it becomes a tax.
narratives.Proposal: Make graphify refresh automatic at the cheapest enforcement point: postMergeLifecycle in src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts. After every merge to main, run `graphify update .` against the project main checkout, then commit (`chore(graphify): refresh after PAN-XXXX`) and push to origin/main. Skip silently when graphify is unavailable or graphify-out/ is missing so projects without graphify aren't broken. Add a `pan doctor` graphify-stale check as a safety net (warns when GRAPH_SUMMARY.md mtime is older than HEAD commit time). CLAUDE.md drops the manual `graphify update .` instruction (now auto-enforced) and keeps the `read on task start` rule (now actually fresh). One refresh helper module, wired into postMergeLifecycle, plus the doctor check, plus a CLAUDE.md tweak.

autoDecisions.summary: Adopted Option A (automate refresh) over Option B (remove rule).
autoDecisions.rationale: The 700-token graph summary IS a real orientation aid when fresh; the failure mode is enforcement, not value. Removing the rule loses signal; automating refresh preserves the signal at minimal ongoing cost.

autoDecisions.summary: Chose postMergeLifecycle hook over PostToolUse hook on Edit|Write.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body recommends this. PostToolUse fires constantly during a session, adding latency to every edit and requiring rate-limit logic. Post-merge fires once per merged issue, is naturally serialized per-issue by _completedPostMerge, runs in the dashboard server's restart process (out-of-band of agent context), and keeps the graph at most one merge stale.

autoDecisions.summary: Extracted refresh into src/lib/graphify/refresh.ts rather than inlining in merge-agent.ts.
autoDecisions.rationale: merge-agent.ts is already 1598 lines and tightly focused on lifecycle orchestration. A separate module isolates the git+graphify shell sequence for unit testing with mocked execAsync, and lets future callers (e.g. close-out, pan doctor --fix) reuse the same helper.

autoDecisions.summary: Inserted refresh between transitionIssueToVerifyingOnMain and compactBeads, not at the end of postMergeLifecycle.
autoDecisions.rationale: Running early in the lifecycle keeps the refresh attached to the issue's verifying-on-main state (so any logged failure is visible against the right issue) and ensures later steps proceed independently if refresh skips or fails.

autoDecisions.summary: Skip silently (info-level log) when graphify is absent or graphify-out/ does not exist; non-fatal warn (logged via logActivity) when push fails after one retry.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue requirement: 'projects without graphify aren't broken'. Failure must never block merge handoff — the doctor check catches sustained refresh failures separately.

autoDecisions.summary: Commit subject: `chore(graphify): refresh after <ISSUE-ID>`. Stage ONLY graphify-out/ (never git add -A). Push directly to origin/main with one fetch+rebase+retry on non-fast-forward.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the issue's template literally. Restricting staging to graphify-out/ prevents the husky commit-msg hook (which guards package.json version bumps) from firing. One retry handles the common race when two merges land in close succession; further retries risk feedback loops.

autoDecisions.summary: All shell-out uses execAsync (promisified exec), not execSync.
autoDecisions.rationale: Repo rule (src/.claude/rules/no-execsync-server.md, PAN-70): server code MUST NOT block the event loop. Refresh runs inside the restarted dashboard server process which serves HTTP/WS concurrently.

autoDecisions.summary: pan doctor compares GRAPH_SUMMARY.md mtime against the local HEAD commit timestamp (not origin/main HEAD).
autoDecisions.rationale: Avoids false positives when a local clone has fetched but not pulled. If the working tree is at HEAD and GRAPH_SUMMARY.md mtime ≥ HEAD commit time, the graph is fresh by definition. Drift relative to origin/main is a separate concern (regular `git pull` solves it).
Acceptance criterion is <10s on this repo. 60s is a generous safety net for larger repos; on timeout, log and skip the commit rather than hang postMergeLifecycle indefinitely.

items.title: Add src/lib/graphify/refresh.ts with refreshGraphify(projectPath, issueId) helper

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/graphify/refresh.ts exporting `refreshGraphify(projectPath: string, issueId: string): Promise<RefreshResult>`. RefreshResult is a discriminated union: { skipped: 'no-graphify-cli' | 'no-graphify-out' | 'no-changes' | 'gitignored' } | { ok: true; commit: string; pushed: boolean } | { ok: false; error: string }. Implementation steps in order: (1) Use `which graphify` via execAsync to detect CLI; return skipped:'no-graphify-cli' if missing. (2) Check `existsSync(join(projectPath, 'graphify-out'))`; return skipped:'no-graphify-out' if absent. (3) Run `graphify update .` via execAsync with cwd=projectPath and 60s timeout. (4) `git add graphify-out/` in projectPath. (5) `git diff --cached --quiet` — if exit 0, return skipped:'no-changes' (or 'gitignored'). (6) `git commit -m "chore(graphify): refresh after ${issueId}"`. (7) `git push origin main`; on non-fast-forward, run `git fetch origin main && git pull --rebase origin main && git push origin main` once. (8) Return ok:true with commit SHA. Wrap each shell-out in try/catch; map failures to RefreshResult.ok=false with descriptive error strings. NO execSync anywhere — repo rule (see hazards). NO `git add -A` — only stage graphify-out/.

subItems.title: Module exports refreshGraphify(projectPath, issueId): Promise<RefreshResult> with the discriminated-union shape described above.
All shell-out uses execAsync from src/lib/exec.ts (or equivalent promisified exec). Grep `execSync` in src/lib/graphify/refresh.ts returns zero matches.
Returns skipped:'no-graphify-cli' when `which graphify` fails. Does not throw, does not write to git.
Returns skipped:'no-graphify-out' when `${projectPath}/graphify-out/` does not exist. Does not throw, does not write to git.
Returns skipped:'no-changes' (or 'gitignored') when `git diff --cached --quiet` exits 0 after staging graphify-out/.

On successful path: commit subject is exactly `chore(graphify): refresh after <ISSUE-ID>` (uppercase issue id passed in). Only files under graphify-out/ are staged — verified by inspecting the commit's changed-file list.
On `git push` non-fast-forward, helper retries once with fetch+rebase+push. Second push failure returns ok:false with error string including 'push failed'. Does not throw.
graphify update invocation has a 60s timeout via execAsync timeout option. On timeout, helper returns ok:false with error 'graphify update timed out'.

items.title: Invoke refreshGraphify from postMergeLifecycle in src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts, after `transitionIssueToVerifyingOnMain(issueId, projectPath)` succeeds (around line 444) and BEFORE the `compactBeads` step (line 463), add a try/catch block that dynamically imports `../graphify/refresh.js`, awaits `refreshGraphify(projectPath, issueId)`, and routes the RefreshResult to appropriate log lines: console.log + logActivity('graphify_refresh', 'Updated graphify-out and pushed commit <sha>') on ok:true; console.log on skipped (info-level, no logActivity entry needed); console.warn + logActivity('graphify_refresh_failed', '...') on ok:false. Wrap the entire block in try/catch so that any thrown error from the helper is downgraded to a warn-level log — postMergeLifecycle never fails because of graphify.

subItems.title: After a successful merge of any issue on a project with graphify installed and graphify-out/ present, postMergeLifecycle invokes refreshGraphify against projectPath.
Successful refresh logs both a console line and a logActivity entry tagged graphify_refresh with the commit SHA.
Skipped refresh (graphify missing or graphify-out missing) logs only a console line (info-level), no logActivity entry, no warn/error.
Failed refresh logs a console.warn AND a logActivity entry tagged graphify_refresh_failed, but postMergeLifecycle continues to the compactBeads step and ultimately marks _completedPostMerge.
Any thrown exception from refreshGraphify is caught at the call site and downgraded to a console.warn — postMergeLifecycle does not throw because of graphify.

items.title: Add graphify-stale check to pan doctor

narrative.Action: Add a `checkGraphifyFreshness(projectPath: string): CheckResult` function to src/cli/commands/doctor.ts (or a small helper module imported there). For each project in projects.yaml, check if `${projectPath}/graphify-out/GRAPH_SUMMARY.md` exists. If absent, skip (no check result emitted). If present, compare its mtime against the timestamp of the local HEAD commit (`git log -1 --format=%ct`). Status: 'ok' (message: 'Fresh — updated since HEAD') if mtime ≥ HEAD commit time; 'warn' (message: 'Stale — N days older than HEAD', fix: 'Run: graphify update . from <projectPath>; or wait for the next merge to refresh automatically') otherwise. Add the call to doctorCommand alongside the other checks. Use execAsync for the git command — no execSync.

subItems.title: `pan doctor` emits a 'Graphify graph freshness' check result for projects that have graphify-out/GRAPH_SUMMARY.md.
Projects without graphify-out/ produce no check result at all (silent skip), so projects that don't use graphify don't see warnings.
When GRAPH_SUMMARY.md mtime ≥ local HEAD commit timestamp, status is 'ok' with message indicating freshness.
When GRAPH_SUMMARY.md mtime < local HEAD commit timestamp, status is 'warn' with message including age and a fix suggestion.
Git command runs via execAsync; grep for execSync in the new check function returns zero matches.

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/graphify/__tests__/refresh.test.ts (vitest). Mock execAsync (or the exec module the helper uses) to script command outcomes per test. Test cases: (1) `which graphify` fails → returns skipped:'no-graphify-cli', no further commands run. (2) graphify present but graphify-out/ missing → returns skipped:'no-graphify-out'. (3) graphify update succeeds, `git diff --cached --quiet` returns exit 0 → returns skipped:'no-changes', no commit. (4) graphify update succeeds, diff non-empty → git add, git commit, git push all called once each in order; returns ok:true with commit SHA. (5) git push returns non-fast-forward exit code → helper calls git fetch, git pull --rebase, git push; on second-push success returns ok:true. (6) Second git push also fails → returns ok:false with 'push failed' in error. (7) graphify update times out (simulate via mock rejecting with timeout error) → returns ok:false with 'graphify update timed out'. (8) graphify update throws unexpectedly → returns ok:false, does not propagate exception. Use vi.useFakeTimers() if any timeout logic is exercised via real-clock delays (per repo rule on retry/Retry-After tests).

subItems.title: Test file src/lib/graphify/__tests__/refresh.test.ts exists with at least 8 test cases covering the scenarios above.
All tests pass under `npm test`.
Tests mock execAsync (or shell entry point) — no real `graphify`, `git`, or filesystem mutations on the project tree during test runs.
The 'push retry on non-fast-forward' test verifies the exact command sequence (git fetch → git pull --rebase → git push) and that the retry happens exactly once.
Any test that exercises timeout/retry timing uses vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync — no real-clock setTimeout delays in the test file (per CLAUDE.md test rule).

items.title: Update CLAUDE.md graphify section to reflect automatic refresh

narrative.Action: In CLAUDE.md (line 553-ish, the `## graphify` section): DROP the bullet `After modifying code, run \`graphify update .\` to keep the graph current (AST-only, no API cost).` REPLACE with a single sentence noting auto-refresh: `The graph is refreshed automatically by the merge agent after every merge to main; agents do not need to run \`graphify update\` manually.` KEEP the other three bullets (read GRAPH_SUMMARY.md on task start; use CLI for cross-module queries; only read source files once you know which ones). No other CLAUDE.md sections change.

subItems.title: The bullet `After modifying code, run graphify update .` is removed from CLAUDE.md.
A new sentence (or bullet) explicitly states that the graph is auto-refreshed by the merge agent after every merge to main.
The other three graphify rules (read on task start, use CLI for cross-module queries, only read source files when known) remain intact.
